volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 1.5.520 - Section 2.2.624_625)

sections.label: Section 1.5.520
sections.original: Servitum tibi me abducito , ni fecero .
sections.translation: Carry me away to be your slave if I don ’ t do it .

sections.label: Section 1.5.521
sections.original: Bene atque amice dicis . † nam nunc , nam meust .
sections.translation: You speak kindly and obligingly ; for at present you are not mine , I suppose .

sections.label: Section 1.5.522
sections.original: Vin etiam dicam quod vos magis miremini ?
sections.translation: Would you like me to tell you , too , what you will still more wonder at ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.523
sections.original: Studeo hercle audire , nam ted ausculto lubens .
sections.translation: Come , then ; i ’ faith , I long to hear it ; I listen to you with pleasure .

sections.label: Section 1.5.524
sections.original: Prius quam ístam pugnam pugnabo , ego etiam prius
sections.translation: Before I fight that battle , I shall first

sections.label: Section 1.5.525
sections.original: dabo aliam pugnam claram et commemorabilem .
sections.translation: fight another battle , famous and memorable .

sections.label: Section 1.5.526
sections.original: Quam pugnam ?
sections.translation: What battle ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.526b
sections.original: Em ab hoc lenone vicino tuo
sections.translation: Why , with the procurer your neighbour ; by means of stratagem and artful tricks , I ’ l cleverly bamboozle the procurer out of this music-girl , with whom your son is so desperately in love ;

sections.label: Section 1.5.530
sections.original: Effectum hoc hodie reddam utrumque ad vesperum .
sections.translation: and I surely will have both of these things effected this very day , before the evening .

sections.label: Section 1.5.531
sections.original: Siquidem ístaec opera , ut praedicas , perfeceris ,
sections.translation: Well , if you accomplish these tasks as you say , you will surpass in might King Agathocles [*] . But if you don ’ t do it , is there any reason why I shouldn ’ t forthwith put you in the treadmill ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.534b
sections.original: Non unum in diem modo ,
sections.translation: Not for one day , but ,

sections.label: Section 1.5.535
sections.original: verum hercle in omnis , quantumst ; sed si effecero ,
sections.translation: i ’ faith , for all , whatever the time . But if I effect it , will you not at once give me the money of your own free will for me to pay to the procurer ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.537b
sections.original: Ius bonum orat Pseudolus ;
sections.translation: Pseudolus is making a fair claim ; say I ’ ll give it .

sections.label: Section 1.5.538b
sections.original: At enim scin quid mihi in mentem venit ?
sections.translation: But still , do you know what comes into my mind ? Suppose they have made an arrangement , Callipho ,

sections.label: Section 1.5.540
sections.original: aut de compecto faciunt consutis dolis ,
sections.translation: among themselves , or are acting in concert , and on a preconcerted plan , to bamboozle me out of the money ?

sections.label: Section 1.5.541b
sections.original: Quis me audacior
sections.translation: Who would be more audacious than myself , if I dared to do such an action ? Well , Simo , if we are thus in collusion , or have ever arranged any plan , do you mark me quite all over with elm-tree stripes [*] , just as when letters are written in a book with a reed .

sections.label: Section 1.5.546
sections.original: Indice ludos nunciam , quando lubet .
sections.translation: Now then , proclaim the games as soon as you please .

sections.label: Section 1.5.547
sections.original: Da in hunc diem operam , Callipho , quaeso mihi ,
sections.translation: Give me your attention , Callipho , I beg you , for this day , so that you may not any way employ yourself upon other business .

sections.label: Section 1.5.549
sections.original: Quin rus ut irem iám heri mecum statueram .
sections.translation: Why , now , I had made up my mind yesterday to go into the country .

sections.label: Section 1.5.550
sections.original: At nunc disturba quas statuisti machinas .
sections.translation: Still , do you now change the plan which you had resolved upon .

sections.label: Section 1.5.551
sections.original: Nunc non abire certum est istac gratia ;
sections.translation: I am now resolved not to go away on account of this ; I have an inclination to be a spectator of your games , Pseudolus ; and if I shall find that he doesn ’ t give you the money which he has promised , rather than it shouldn ’ t be done , I ’ ll give it .

sections.label: Section 1.5.555
sections.original: Non demutabo .
sections.translation: I shall not change my purpose .

sections.label: Section 1.5.555b
sections.original: Namque edepol , si non dabis ,
sections.translation: Because , by my faith , if you don ’ t give it , you shall be dunned for it with clamour great and plenteous . Come , now , move yourselves off hence into the house this instant , and in turn give room for my tricks .

sections.label: Section 1.5.559
sections.original: Fiat , geratur mos tibi .
sections.translation: Be it so .

sections.label: Section 1.5.559b
sections.original: Sed te volo
sections.translation: But I want you

sections.label: Section 1.5.560
sections.original: domi usque adesse .
sections.translation: to keep close at home .

sections.label: Section 1.5.560b
sections.original: Quin tibi hanc operam dico .—
sections.translation: Well , that assistance I promise you .

sections.label: Section 1.5.561
sections.original: At ego ad forum ibo . iam hic ero .—
sections.translation: But I shall be off to the Forum . I ’ ll be back here presently .

sections.label: Section 1.5.561b
sections.original: Actutum redi .
sections.translation: Be back directly . ( To the AUDIENCE .) I have a suspicion , now , that you are suspecting that I have been promising these so great exploits to these persons for the purpose of amusing you , while I am acting this play ,

sections.label: Section 1.5.565
sections.original: neque sim facturus quod facturum dixeram .
sections.translation: and that I shall not do that which I said I will do . I will not change my design ; so far as that then I know for certain ; by what means I ’ m to carry it out not at all do I know as yet ; only this , that so it shall be . For he that appears upon the stage in a new character , him it befits to bring something that is new .

sections.label: Section 1.5.570
sections.original: si id facere nequeat , det locum illi qui queat .
sections.translation: If he cannot do that , let him give place to him who can . I am inclined to go hence into the house for some little time , while I summon together [*] all my stratagems in my mind . Meanwhile this piper shall entertain you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.574
sections.original: Pro Iúppiter , ut mihi , quídquid ago , lepide ómnia prospereque éveniunt :
sections.translation: O Jupiter , whatever I undertake , how cleverly and how fortunately does it befal me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.575
sections.original: neque quód dubitem neque quód timeam , meo in péctore conditumst cónsilium .
sections.translation: Not any plan is there stored up in my breast that I can hesitate upon or be afraid of . But it is folly to entrust a bold exploit to a timorous heart ; for all things are just as you make them , so as you make them of importance . Now in my breast have I already so prepared my forces —

sections.label: Section 2.1.580
sections.original: duplicís triplicis dolos pérfidias , ut , ubíquomque hostibus cóngrediar
sections.translation: double , aye , threefold stratagems , that when I engage with the enemy , relying upon the merits , I say , of my forefathers , and on my own industry and tricking propensity for mischief , I may easily conquer , and easily spoil my antagonists by my contrivances .

sections.label: Section 2.1.584
sections.original: núnc inimicum ego húnc communem meum átque vostrorum ómnium ,
sections.translation: Now will I adroitly batter down this Ballio [*] , the common foe of me and all of you ; only lend me your attention . Now will I forthwith draw out my legions against this old town . If I take it , I shall make it a pleasant matter for the citizens : I ’ ll load and fill myself , and my allies as well , with booty from it . I shall strike terror and fright into my enemies , so that they may know of what race I was born .

sections.label: Section 2.1.590
sections.original: éo sum genere gnátus : magna mé facinora décet efficere ,
sections.translation: Great exploits it befits me to perform , which long after may bespeak fame for me . But whom do I see here ? Who ’ s this low fellow that ’ s presented before my eyes ? I should like to know why he ’ s come here with his sword : I ’ troth , now then I ’ ll lie here in ambush for him , to see the business that he ’ s about .

sections.label: Section 2.2.594_595
sections.original: Hi lóci sunt atque hae régiones quae mi áb ero sunt demónstratae ,
sections.translation: This is the place , and this the spot , which was pointed out to me by my master , according as I form a judgment from my eyesight . For my master , the Captain , told me to this effect , that the house was the seventh from the gate , in which lives the person to whom he requested me to carry the token and this silver * * * * * I could vastly wish that some one would inform me where this Ballio , the procurer , lives .

sections.label: Section 2.2.600
sections.original: St , tace , tace , meus hic est homo , ni omnes di atque homines deserunt .
sections.translation: Hist ! Silence ! This man is mine , unless all Gods and men forsake me . Now have I need of a new plan ; this new scheme is suddenly presented to me . This I prefer to my former one ; that I shall dismiss , which , before , I had commenced to carry into effect . By my troth , I ’ ll then work this military messenger that ’ s just arrived .

sections.label: Section 2.2.604
sections.original: óstium pultábo atque intus évocabo aliquém foras .
sections.translation: I ’ ll knock at the door , and call some one out of doors from within .

sections.label: Section 2.2.605
sections.original: Quisquis es , compendium ego te facere pultandi volo ;
sections.translation: Whoever you are , I wish you to spare your knocking ; for I ’ ve just come out of doors , I , the spokesman and the defender of the door .

sections.label: Section 2.2.607
sections.original: Tune es Ballio ?
sections.translation: Are you Ballio ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.607b
sections.original: Immo vero ego eius sum Subballio .
sections.translation: Why , no ; but I ’ m the deputy-Ballio [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.608
sections.original: Quid istuc verbist ?
sections.translation: What means that expression ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.608b
sections.original: Condus promus sum , procurator peni .
sections.translation: I ’ m his butler-steward [*] ; the caterer for his larder .

sections.label: Section 2.2.609
sections.original: Quasi te dicas atriensem .
sections.translation: As though you were to say , you are his chamberlain [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.2.609b
sections.original: Immo atriensi ego impero .
sections.translation: No ; I ’ m above his chamberlain .

sections.label: Section 2.2.610
sections.original: Quid tu , servon es an liber ?
sections.translation: What are you , slave or free man ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.610b
sections.original: Nunc quidem etiam servio .
sections.translation: Why , at present , I ’ m still a slave .

sections.label: Section 2.2.611
sections.original: Ita videre , et non videre dignus qui liber sies .
sections.translation: So you seem to be ; and you don ’ t look to be one worthy to be free .

sections.label: Section 2.2.612
sections.original: Non soles respicere te , quom dicis iniuste alteri ?
sections.translation: Ain ’ t you in the habit of looking at yourself when you abuse another person ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.613
sections.original: Hunc hominem malum esse oportet .
sections.translation: This must be a roguish fellow .

sections.label: Section 2.2.613b
sections.original: Di me servant atque amant ,
sections.translation: The Gods protect and favour me ! for this is my anvil : this day will I hammer out thence full many a device .

sections.label: Section 2.2.615
sections.original: Quid illic secum solus loquitur ?
sections.translation: Why is he talking to himself alone ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.615b
sections.original: Quid ais tu , adulescens ?
sections.translation: How say you , young man —?

sections.label: Section 2.2.615c
sections.original: Quid est ?
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.616
sections.original: Esne tu an non es ab illo milite Macedonio ,
sections.translation: Are you , or are you not , from that Captain of Macedonia ? The servant of him , II mean , who bought a damsel of us here , who gave fifteen silver minae to the procurer , my master , and is still owing five ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.619b
sections.original: Sum . sed ubi tu me novisti gentium
sections.translation: I am . But where in the world have you ever known me ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.620
sections.original: aut vidisti aut conlocutu ’ (s e) s ? nam equidem Athenas antidhac
sections.translation: or have ever seen or spoken to me ? For in fact , before this day , I never was at Athens , nor did I ever before this day behold you with my eyes * * * * * * * * * *

sections.label: Section 2.2.622
sections.original: Quia videre inde esse ; nam olim quom abiit , argento haec dies
sections.translation: Because you seem likely to be from him ; for at the time when he went away , this was the day appointed for the money , on which he was to pay it to us , and he has not brought it as yet .

sections.label: Section 2.2.624_625
sections.original: Immo adest .
sections.translation: Yes , here it is .
